title: Treasure Island
author: Stevenson, Robert Louis
summary: Jim Hawkins recounts how an old sea‑dog named the captain took up lodging at his family’s Admiral Benbow inn, bringing a mysterious sea‑chest and frightening stories of pirates, the dead man’s chest, and a one‑legged seafaring man. Squire Trelawney and Dr. Livesey soon join Jim’s account as strange visitors and ominous signs accumulate: the black spot, sea songs, secret papers, and talk of treasure. Fear, loyalty, and appetite for adventure lead them toward a voyage and a treasure hunt. The narrative promises treachery, shipboard life, and contests between honest men and veteran buccaneers, and a race to recover buried gold.

characters.name: Jim Hawkins
characters.personality: curious, observant, courageous narrator and innkeeper’s son
characters.lifespan: survives to tell the tale; becomes principal chronicler of the events

characters.name: Squire Trelawney
characters.personality: wealthy, impulsive, enthusiastic about adventure and fitting out the voyage
characters.lifespan: organizes the expedition; remains alive after the events

characters.name: Dr. Livesey
characters.personality: calm, rational, principled, medically skilled and courageous
characters.lifespan: survives and aids in narrative and leadership

characters.name: the captain (the old sea‑dog)
characters.personality: secretive, terrifying storyteller, domineering when drunk
characters.lifespan: his presence catalyzes events at the Admiral Benbow; he is a central early figure (dies or is removed in the course of the story)

characters.name: Black Dog
characters.personality: sly, menacing seafarer who confronts the captain
characters.lifespan: appears and vanishes in violent confrontation with the captain

characters.name: Blind man (the Blind Man / Blind Pew)
characters.personality: sinister, threatening, connected with pirate bands
characters.lifespan: appears to deliver ominous signs such as the black spot; meets a violent fate in the narrative

characters.name: Captain Silver (Long John Silver)
characters.personality: charismatic, cunning, persuasive, double‑faced — companionable and treacherous
characters.lifespan: becomes the principal pirate antagonist; survives much of the action though ultimately defeated or set aside

characters.name: Israel Hands
characters.personality: seasoned seaman, rough, competent; notable among the crew
characters.lifespan: active in shipboard conflicts; suffers violent consequences in the tale

characters.name: Ben Gunn (the man of the island)
characters.personality: eccentric, resourceful, affected by long isolation
characters.lifespan: found on the island and plays a role in the treasure hunt; survives the core events

characters.name: Captain Smollett
characters.personality: professional, strict, suspicious of improvised crews and quick to duty
characters.lifespan: commands the vessel during the voyage and survives the central action

characters.name: Mr. Hawkins (Jim’s father)
characters.lifespan: falls into illness and dies during early events at the Admiral Benbow

characters.name: Mrs. Hawkins (Jim’s mother)
characters.personality: domestic, hospitable, runs the inn with her husband
characters.lifespan: survives the initial incidents and helps care for guests

characters.name: Old Taylor (Taylor, the gardener)
characters.lifespan: appears in early domestic scenes; survives early events

characters.name: the man who trundled the barrow
characters.personality: minor, functional role delivering the captain’s chest

characters.name: the hawker
characters.personality: minor, provides goods (stockings) to the captain

locations.name: Spanish Main (general Caribbean coast region, representative point Cartagena, Colombia)
